@metadata.note: Translator documentation. Each key maps to a short note for translators explaining intent, parameters, and where the string appears.

header.buttons.add_function: Header button. Opens the search-and-pin modal. The leading + is literal punctuation signalling additivity.
header.buttons.import: Header button. Opens the import-existing-Z14 modal.
header.buttons.declare_shell: Header button. Opens the declare-function-shell modal.
header.buttons.run: Header button. Runs the composition against the Wikifunctions API.
header.buttons.export: Header button. Opens the export-ZObject-JSON modal.
header.status.empty: Status line shown when no shell has been declared. Double quotes are around UI button names.
header.status.editing_impl: Suffix on the shell status line when a composition was imported from a specific Z14. $1 is the Z14 ZID.
header.status.editing_impl_labeled: Same as editing_impl but with a human label. $1 = Z14 ZID, $2 = Z14 label.
header.example_picker_default: First option in the Load Example dropdown. Ellipsis indicates more options follow.
shell_modal.title: Title of the shell-declaration modal.
shell_modal.hint: Brief explanation of what the shell is. Contains HTML: <code> and angle-bracket-escaped < >.
shell_modal.invalid_zid: Alert shown when user enters a non-ZID in the function ZID field. $1 = the bad input.
shell_modal.confirm_clear: Confirmation shown when saving a shell change that will invalidate existing arg-ref blocks.
import_modal.title: Title of the import-existing-composition modal.
import_modal.zid_label: Label for the ZID input field. Colon is literal.
import_modal.invalid_zid: Error shown when user pastes something that isn't a ZID. $1 = the bad input. Phrasing intentionally informal.
import_modal.confirm_replace: Confirmation when importing will clobber current workspace contents.
run_modal.scope_banner: Label prefix when the run is scoped to a subtree (via right-click on a block). Followed by the subtree's rendered toString().
run_modal.test_label: Label for the test-prefill dropdown. Used above the args list in the Run modal.
result_modal.raw_details: Summary text for the collapsible showing the raw Z22 envelope.
export_modal.hint: Single-sentence explanation of where to paste the exported JSON. <code> and <em> are allowed.
search_modal.hint: Describes the search-and-pin flow. <strong> wraps the category name.
search_modal.status.count: Result count status. $1 = result count; the PLURAL magic selects singular/plural for English; translators should adapt using their language's CLDR rules (all banana-i18n plural forms available).
search_modal.zid_is_impl: Shown when the user searches for a Z14 ZID. $1 = typed Z14 ZID, $2 = HTML link to the parent Z8. Explains that we fetched the parent function and show it below for one-click pinning.
fill_slots.title: Title of the fill-slots-from-test modal. $1 = function label.
fill_slots.hint: Explains that the modal pulls values from the function's attached Z20 testers. $1 = function ZID.
slot_picker.title: Title of the slot-picker. $1 = argument label of the target slot.
slot_picker.hint: Explains the filter rule. $1 = human-readable type + ZID combo.
context_menu.fill_slot: Right-click menu item on a function block with empty value inputs. $1 = argument label.
context_menu.peek_inside: Right-click menu item on function-call blocks and Z-ref literals. Opens a read-only popover that shows the referenced function's composition so the user can drill into its internals.
context_menu.inline_composition: Right-click menu item on function-call blocks. Replaces the block with its own composition body, substituting the call-site arguments for the function's Z18 arg-refs. The resulting tree is semantically equivalent but one level deeper.
inline.err.not_function_block: Error shown if the block the user right-clicked isn't a function-call block. Should not normally occur because the menu item is only attached to wf_Z### blocks.
inline.err.not_z8: Error shown when the referenced ZID doesn't resolve to a function definition. $1 = the ZID.
inline.err.no_composition: Error shown when the function has no Z14 composition implementation (all implementations are code-based). $1 = the function ZID.
inline.err.empty_slot: Error shown when an argument slot on the call-site block is empty. $1 = argument label, $2 = argument key (e.g. Z21216K1).
inline.err.signatures_failed: Error shown when the inlined composition references functions we couldn't look up. $1 = comma-separated ZIDs.
inline.err.unexpected_argref: Error shown when the composition contains a Z18 arg-ref whose key doesn't match any of the callee's shell arguments — indicates a malformed implementation. $1 = the offending key.
inline.err.unexpected: Error shown for any uncategorised failure during inlining. $1 = the raw error message.
peek.loading: Loading placeholder inside the Peek popover while a function's definition is fetched. $1 = ZID.
peek.impl_label: Small caption above the composition body indicating which Z14 implementation is being shown. $1 is an HTML <a> linking to the Z14's Wikifunctions page.
peek.no_composition: Shown when the function has only code-based implementations (no Z14 composition).
peek.is_z14: Shown when the user tries to Peek into a ZID that is itself an implementation (Z14) rather than a function definition (Z8).
peek.not_function: Shown when the ZID resolves to something other than a Z8. $1 = the Z-type ZID found.
peek.empty_list: Placeholder shown in the tree for an empty typed list (just the type-head marker, no items).
peek.drill_title: Hover tooltip on the small ▸ button next to each function-call node. Clicking expands that callee's own composition inline.
error.no_root_block: Emission error: workspace is empty or has nothing that outputs a value.
error.multiple_roots: Emission error: user has multiple unattached value blocks. $1 = the count.
error.shell_def_empty: Emission error: shell-def frame has no composition plugged into its body slot.
footer.source: Page footer. Contains an HTML anchor linking to the zblocks GitHub repository. Translators: keep the … tag intact; translate only the surrounding text (the URL itself should not be translated).
